reasoning: The Lean formalization precisely captures the informal statement. The set S is encoded via the two consecutive-difference equalities (b-a = c-b and c-b = d-c), which is exactly the arithmetic progression condition on the entries read in order a, b, c, d. The exponent k ranges over ℕ (from `Monoid.npow` on matrices), and k > 1 matches "integer k > 1" since matrix powers are only naturally defined for nonnegative integers and positive values. The solution set encodes the two families: α·J (all entries equal α) and β·[[-3,-1],[1,3]] (β times the centered AP matrix). I verified mathematically using Cayley-Hamilton with M = μJ + fL that these are exactly the solutions: when f=0 all powers stay in S, when μ=0 odd powers stay in S, and when both are nonzero the recurrence a_n = (λ₁ⁿ-λ₂ⁿ)/(λ₁-λ₂) is never zero for n≥1. The iff correctly characterizes "Find all M" with the required conjunction M ∈ S ∧ ∃ k > 1, M^k ∈ S.
